case_name: PREFERRED WOMEN'S HEALTHCARE LLC v. JASON A. SAIN

summary.brief: PREFERRED WOMEN'S HEALTHCARE LLC v. JASON A. SAIN. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Debbie Sain became pregnant in early 2012, when she was 36 years old. Ultrasounds performed by two different PWH sonographers on April 3, 10, and 20, 2012, revealed a mass in Debbie’s right adnexa.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On January 3, 2013, Debbie visited an emergency room, complaining of abdominal pain and a fever. When a CT scan showed a mass in her abdomen, she underwent emergency surgery performed by Dr. Dickerson.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. DeSimone and a second expert witness, James Quirk, M.D., testified that various medical professionals’ failures to timely intervene when Debbie’s right adnexal mass was discovered likely caused or substantially hastened her death.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: The jury found in favor of Jason and against the appellants and awarded Jason a total of $8,520,182.60 in damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The verdict form therefore does not unequivocally establish that the jury found 'in favor of the Appellants' on Jason’s wrongful death claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Judgment affirmed. Doyle, P. J., and Gobeil, J., concur fully as to Division One, and concur in judgment only as to Division Two.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented to the emergency room with abdominal pain and fever. Previous ultrasounds during pregnancy revealed a right adnexal mass.

requestables.name: Ultrasound
requestables.description: Ultrasound of the abdomen and pelvis

reveal.result_summary: Right adnexal mass measuring between 5.7 and 6.27 cm
reveal.clinical_significance: Consistent with a dermoid, which typically will not resolve on its own
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further investigation and monitoring

reveal.result_summary: Mass in the abdomen, measuring 11 cm and ruptured
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates a significant increase in size and rupture, requiring emergency intervention

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with abdominal pain and fever

key_events.event: Patient presents to the emergency room

timeline_phases.name: Diagnostic Workup
timeline_phases.description: Imaging and assessment of the adnexal mass

key_events.event: CT scan performed revealing ruptured mass

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Surgery
timeline_phases.description: Surgical intervention to remove the ruptured mass

key_events.event: Emergency surgery performed by Dr. Dickerson

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with a known adnexal mass during pregnancy
decision_points.prompt: What should be done about the adnexal mass identified in the ultrasound?

options.description: Monitor the mass with regular follow-up and imaging
options.clinical_reasoning: Mass was not included in the problem list or followed up

options.description: Inform the patient and consider surgical intervention
options.clinical_reasoning: Early intervention could have prevented complications

actual_action_defendant.description: Mass was not monitored or included in the problem list

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been informed and mass monitored or removed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated early intervention was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to monitor or inform the patient about the mass led to its rupture and subsequent complications
explanation.what_should_have_happened: The mass should have been monitored and potentially removed before it ruptured
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed squamous cell carcinoma from a mature teratoma and died in December 2013

key_findings: Failure to inform and monitor the adnexal mass led to its rupture and worsened prognosis

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to monitor and inform about the adnexal mass
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Early intervention could have prevented the rupture and cancer progression

factual_timeline.event: Ultrasound revealed a right adnexal mass
Debbie presented to the emergency room with abdominal pain and fever

diagnoses.diagnosis: Squamous cell carcinoma from a mature teratoma

treatments.treatment: Emergency surgery to remove the mass

allegations.allegation: Failure to monitor and inform about the adnexal mass

court_findings.finding: Defendants were negligent in failing to monitor and inform about the adnexal mass
